topic.what: Machine-checked composition theorems for arithmetic masking over prime fields in post-quantum cryptographic hardware (NTT-based PQC)
topic.how: Proves renewal theorem and positive/negative composition theorems for PF-PINI gadgets with/without fresh masking; formalizes 18 theorems in Lean 4 with zero sorry stubs; bridges algebraic and hardware-faithful Barrett reduction models
topic.why_matters: Arithmetic masking over Z_q lacks composition theory unlike Boolean masking (NI/SNI/PINI); enables secure composition of masked gadgets in NTT pipelines; provides first formal verification framework for post-quantum hardware security

characteristics.what_it_measures: PF-PINI parameter bound for composed pipeline
characteristics.value: k2 with fresh masking (Stage 1 multiplicity erased)
characteristics.evidence: Renewal theorem: exactly q mask pairs per target intermediate value

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of mask pairs producing given intermediate wire value
characteristics.value: q with fresh masking; up to k1 without
characteristics.evidence: Four-line Lean 4 proof of renewal lemma
characteristics.context: Renewal theorem creates necessary condition for DPA when bound achieved without fresh masking

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of machine-checked theorems in Lean 4
characteristics.evidence: 3 combinatorial lemmas, 4 positive-composition, 4 negative-composition, 5 Adams Bridge, 2 algebraic-hardware bridge

characteristics.what_it_measures: Bits of information leaked per probed wire under PF-PINI(2)
characteristics.evidence: PF-PINI(2) phenomenon: some output values hit by two masks, others by one
characteristics.context: Barrett reduction has input-dependent branching breaking uniform mask distribution

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proof of algebraic equals hardware-faithful Barrett reduction
characteristics.context: Enables application of PF-PINI(2) bound to actual hardware implementations

characteristics.vs_baseline: No prior formalization for arithmetic masking composition
characteristics.context: First machine-checked proofs in this domain

good_for: Verifying security of NTT-based PQC hardware implementations
Composing arithmetic masking gadgets in multi-stage pipelines
Formal diagnosis of architectural flaws in PQC accelerators
Providing machine-checked guarantees for side-channel resistance
requires: Prime field arithmetic (Z_q for prime q)
Fresh random masking between pipeline stages for renewal
Specification of PF-PINI parameters k1, k2 for each stage

novelty: First machine-checked composition theorems for arithmetic masking over prime fields
Renewal argument: fresh masking completely erases Stage 1 security parameter from composed output
Algebraic-hardware bridge proving Barrett reduction equivalence for formal verification
First formal diagnosis of Adams Bridge PQC accelerator architectural flaw (matches 3 prior empirical analyses)
open_problems: Extension to Montgomery reduction with analogous composition theory
Integration with existing automated verification tools (maskVerif, SILVER)
Practical guidelines for fresh mask insertion in large NTT pipelines
